item.title: I like this!
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for the upvote ^^/

Well already noticed, just waiting for the end of JamCon to make edits

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I hate it. +1

I suggest reformatting the research log collapsible, as if you glance at the last line of the article it undermines the last line of the log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: Changed the image because the first one wasn't CC compliant, source here

https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/e26457b1-b278-42d6-8efc-4d64a5392961

Anyway, anyone else mildly discomfited by the thought of buying a bunch of bananas and finding a spider with it because you saw a picture or a gif once? This is so much worse.

Explanation if you want
I'm sorry if you wanted to drink hot cocoa

the hot cocoa packets are spider egg sacs, the spider eggs are the cocoa powder granules, they're so unusually durable they have to hatch via dust explosion, which 'coincidentally' leaves them with a source of food (their egg sac and unhatched siblings are also an excellent source of food)

tfw you just want to drink hot cocoa but you get exploded by the miracle of baby spider birth
